name: Razorpay gRPC Security Layer
scenario: Razorpay's internal gRPC services are currently using plaintext with no authentication. A security audit found that any pod on the cluster can call any gRPC endpoint. Build a complete security layer: TLS, JWT auth, RBAC, rate limiting, and audit logging.

core.description: GrpcTokenExtractor @Service: extract 'authorization' Metadata key. Throws Status.UNAUTHENTICATED if missing or not 'Bearer ' prefix. Returns raw token string.
JwtServerInterceptor implements ServerInterceptor + @GrpcGlobalServerInterceptor: validate token, close call with UNAUTHENTICATED on failure. Pass to next.startCall() on success.
RbacChecker @Service: Set<String> adminOnlyMethods. check(AuthContext, methodName) throws Status.PERMISSION_DENIED if MERCHANT tries admin method.
GrpcRateLimitInterceptor @GrpcGlobalServerInterceptor: Bucket4j per merchantId. 100 RPCs/minute. Status.RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED when exhausted.
GrpcAuditService @Service: log(method, merchantId, statusCode, latencyMs). @Async. JPA entity GrpcAuditLog.
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): valid token passes, invalid token UNAUTHENTICATED, rate limit RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, MERCHANT on admin method PERMISSION_DENIED. All pass.
README: explains interceptor order (RateLimit → Auth → RBAC → Logging) and why.

bonus.description: Context propagation: Context.Key<AuthContext> AUTH_CONTEXT. Interceptor sets it after validation. Service method reads it. Test: context available in service after interceptor.

mTLS service identity: ServiceIdentity.parse(certificateCN) extracts serviceName/pod/namespace. ServiceAuthValidator checks against ALLOWED_SERVICES set. Tests: allowed passes, unknown PERMISSION_DENIED.
application.yml TLS config: grpc.server.security.enabled=true, certificate-chain, private-key paths (commented for test). Client TlsChannelCredentials.create() example.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Return empty Listener after call.close(): after closing the call with a status, you MUST return a no-op Listener (new ServerCall.Listener<>() {}) — otherwise the framework may try to process further events on the closed call. UNAUTHENTICATED vs PERMISSION_DENIED: UNAUTHENTICATED = can't verify identity (no token, invalid token, expired token). PERMISSION_DENIED = identity verified but insufficient role. Metadata.Key.of('authorization'): key name is lowercase (gRPC HTTP/2 canonicalization). Status.RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED: gRPC's equivalent of HTTP 429 (rate limit). Rate limiter order: put rate limiting BEFORE auth in the interceptor chain — avoids expensive JWT parsing when limit is already exhausted.
